overview.headline: Connect the data estate, catalog and enrich it, map lineage end to end, and hand over the findings.
overview.background: BPC connects the source systems, cloud platform, and models the assessment runs on, ingests metadata, enriches it with usage context, maps end-to-end lineage, and packages findings and recommendations for handover.
overview.outcome: Findings, lineage, and recommendations are delivered as final outputs the client owns.

layers.name: Infrastructure
layers.summary: Every connection the assessment runs on, categorized into Sources, Cloud, and LLM, with live connectivity and usage.

subparts.name: Sources
subparts.desc: Source systems the assessment reads from.

subparts.name: LLM
subparts.desc: Models driving ingestion, scoring, and classification.

layers.name: Collection
layers.summary: Discovery and metadata ingestion: domain intelligence, an automated catalog, enrichment, and transformation analysis.

subparts.name: Domain Intelligence
subparts.desc: Business domain context and data ownership captured with subject-matter experts.

intel.title: BPC inference
intel.note: Inferred by BPC from schema, table names, and value distributions - no SME input.

items.text: Operating model is semiconductor distribution: order-to-cash spans Quotes, Orders, Allocations, and Shipments with a design-win / registration tier ahead of revenue.
Inventory is lot- and wafer-traceable - part, lot, and date-code columns run through the stock and shipment tables, implying strict traceability and RMA handling.
Demand is highly cyclical - order and backlog volumes swing with a ~6-9 month lead-time signal, so allocation and backlog tables are the ones that drive planning.
Customer, contact, and rep-commission tables carry PII and pricing sensitivity - governs Silver-layer masking and access control.

subparts.name: Automated Data Catalog
subparts.desc: Automated ingestion of tables, schemas, columns, and sizes.

subparts.name: Data Enrichment
subparts.desc: Usage patterns, access tools, and frequency from log analysis.

intel.title: BPC data annotation
intel.note: Business meaning BPC attached to each asset, with the evidence it used to decide.

items.note: Core revenue-grain fact - one row per shipped part/lot line.
items.why: Queried by 16 BI reports/day, joins cleanly to DimPart, DimCustomer and DimDateCode, freshness under 1h.

items.note: Open backlog and allocation by part and customer - the planning core.
items.why: Drives demand and lead-time planning; downstream of Orders and Inventory, feeds 5 allocation marts.
Contains contact name, phone, email and commission rate; referenced by 9 marts - governance flag set.

items.note: Superseded staging copy from the legacy AX feed.
items.why: Zero reads in 90 days, no downstream lineage, duplicates Orders at coarser grain.

subparts.name: Transformation
subparts.desc: Pipeline parsing and ETL / database assessment of transformation logic.

meaning.title: What does the data mean?
meaning.subtitle: BPC reads the catalog and answers in business terms - not just what the tables are, but what they say about the business.
meaning.author: BPC · Business Process Co-Pilot

questions.q: What business is this data describing?
questions.a: A semiconductor components distributor. The data captures the full quote-to-cash cycle - design registrations, quotes, orders, lot-traceable inventory, allocation, and shipment - across the part catalog and the active customer / rep book.

In the shipment-line and backlog-allocation grain. A small set of high-volume parts and key accounts drives most revenue, and the part / lot / date-code keys are what tie inventory to shipments - so those joins are the ones worth conforming to Gold first.

questions.q: What does the data warn us about?
questions.a: Lead-time cyclicality and sensitive data. Backlog swings on a 6-9 month lead-time signal, so allocation freshness matters, and customer-contact, pricing and rep-commission fields carry PII and margin sensitivity that must be masked before the Silver layer.

layers.name: Unification
layers.summary: End-to-end lineage across datasets and reference systems, with ownership mapping and manual additions where automation is limited.

subparts.name: End-to-End Visualization
subparts.desc: Lineage from upstream sources through transformations to downstream consumption.

subparts.name: Manual Additions
subparts.desc: Analyst-added lineage where API or metadata access is limited.

topSignal.title: Signal that matters most
topSignal.subtitle: Across the lineage, ownership, and manual-addition work, BPC ranks which signal to act on first for the classification decision.
topSignal.author: BPC · Business Process Co-Pilot

primary.why: The number of certified consumers hanging off a dataset predicts its Gold candidacy better than row count or size. High-fan-out nodes (FactShipmentLine, FactBacklogAllocation) are where conforming pays back first.

ranked.why: An unowned dataset can't be promoted or governed - 20% still unowned is the gating risk for sign-off.
21 gaps where automated lineage stops; each hides possible orphan or broken-dependency risk until an analyst confirms.
19 reference sets feed many marts - stale part / customer master ripples widely but changes slowly.

layers.name: Presentation
layers.summary: The face of the assessment: classification, alignment, simulation, backlog, and enablement, packaged for the client to act and own.

subparts.name: Classification & Alignment
subparts.desc: Dataset classification and current-state alignment baseline.

subparts.name: Impact & Backlog
subparts.desc: Cost/performance simulation and prioritized transition backlog.

deliverables.name: Lineage & Dependency Map
deliverables.delayReason: Source SME sessions slipped two weeks; lineage inputs for the mapping deliverable arrived late, pushing sign-off past the Week 6 gate.

bpcProcessing.title: How BPC processed this estate
bpcProcessing.subtitle: The Business Process Co-Pilot runs every asset through the same pipeline. This is what it did with the semiconductor-distribution catalog.
bpcProcessing.author: BPC · Business Process Co-Pilot

stages.name: Ingest
stages.detail: Read schemas, columns, and row counts from every connected source.

stages.name: Infer
stages.detail: Derived the business model and domain map from names and value distributions.

stages.name: Annotate
stages.detail: Tagged PII, revenue-grain facts, and deprecation candidates with reasoning.

stages.name: Classify
stages.detail: Scoring each dataset to Bronze / Silver / Gold against usage and lineage.

stages.name: Recommend
stages.detail: Prioritized transition backlog and executive recommendations.

confidence.label: Model confidence on this estate
confidence.detail: Weighted across inference, annotation, and classification passes.
